homepage.title: Baroness Tina Stowell - Corporate Speaker Adviser
homepage.meta_description: Baroness Tina Stowell – Corporate Speaker & Adviser. Inspiring leadership, governance & communications for modern organisations.
homepage.text_excerpt: Baroness Tina Stowell - Corporate Speaker Adviser Home About Keynote Speaker Media & Publications Podcast & Pamphlet Contact Home About Keynote Speaker Media & Publications Podcast & Pamphlet Contact Bridging divides and getting results in a low trust world Bridging divides and getting results in a low trust world Speaker. Adviser. Non-Exec. Speaker. Adviser. Non-Exec. Bridging divides and getting results in a low trust world Faced with political and technological disruption, and in a world of low trust and high demands, how can leaders bridge divides and make change happen? Tina Stowell has spent a career bringing unlikely people together to achieve unexpected results – receiving numerous awards for her work. With pressure on leaders so high and trust harder to gain and easier to lose, she now shares her experiences as a keynote speaker, helping others to do the same. Watch Tina’s Video About Tina Tina Stowell was a working-class girl who left school at sixteen, determined to make her way as a secretary. She went on to become a Cabinet Minister and Leader of the House of Lords. The real story isn’t where she began, but why she succeeded and how far it’s taken her. A t every stage of her career , she’s fixed tough problems and got things done by understanding people. Respecting their perspectives. Recognising their anxieties. Finding common ground. From Downing Street to the BBC To leading major institutions under pressure and scrutiny From passing Equal Marriage into law By taking people seriously, she’s bridged divides, earned their trust, built coalitions and got results that defied expectations. READ FULL BIOGRAPHY Compelling Speaker At events and conferences, she brings straight-talking insights, real stories, and practical lessons leaders can use to: understand the source of people’s distrust, apply that understanding to make a persuasive case for change, and unite people to deliver results. More About Tina’s Keynote Speaker Services Compelling Speaker At events and conferences, she brings straight-talking insights, real stories, and practical lessons leaders can use to: understand the source of people’s distrust, apply that understanding to make a persuasive case for change, and unite people to deliver results. More About Tina’s Keynote Speaker Services Some Testimonials “Tina Stowell is an excellent public speaker… she has spoken several times, to great acclaim, at the annual Battle of Ideas festival…. She is always meticulously-well prepared and researched but has an accessible and engaging style that draws you in… Highly professional, concise, great fun but serious – I highly recommend Tina.” Claire Fox Media Commentator, Director of the Academy of Ideas & host of Battle of Ideas Festival “Baroness Stowell has supported Deloitte and Enders Analysis at the Media and Telecoms Conference and related events with excellent regulatory and market insight, sharing her deep industry understanding and engagement with CEO level audiences from across the TMT sectors. A big thank you.” Jolyon Barker Deloitte Global Managing Principal Global Technology, Media & Telecommunications (TMT) Industry group “… At our AI event in Cambridge, Baroness Stowell delivered an exceptional speech. Attendees commended her profound knowledge and insightful perspectives, which not only captivated the audience but also fostered valuable discussions.” Heba Bevan OBE Founder and CEO UtterBerry & Chair of the Netherlands British Chamber of Commerce VIEW MORE TESTIMONIALS Would You Like to sign up for the Tina Stowell Newsletter? pages.title: About Tina Stowell | Baroness Stowell of Beeston MBE
pages.h1: The Rt Hon Baroness Stowell of Beeston MBE
pages.meta_description: About Tina Stowell, A peer in the House of Lords since 2011, she has served as Cabinet Minister, Charity Commission Chair, and led major reforms in equal marriage, press freedom, digital markets, and the charity sector.
headings: h3: Please get in touch to book Tina or to discuss your event.
pages.text_excerpt: About Tina Stowell | Baroness Stowell of Beeston MBE Home About Keynote Speaker Media & Publications Podcast & Pamphlet Contact Home About Keynote Speaker Media & Publications Podcast & Pamphlet Contact The Rt Hon Baroness Stowell of Beeston MBE Tina Stowell is a member of the House of Lords and former Cabinet Minister whose distinguished career has encompassed senior roles in government, Parliament, broadcasting, and public service. Tina was born and brought up in Beeston, just outside Nottingham. Her Dad was a painter & decorator and her Mum worked at the local factory. She attended a local comprehensive, gained her secretarial qualifications and A’ Levels at the local FE college, and moved to London aged 18 after accepting an offer from the Ministry of Defence to join the civil service (though she had assumed when applying it would mean working on a military base). What followed was in large part because of how she applied the sensibilities she grew up with to fixing tough problems. She has come a long way, and making the leap from secretary to executive wasn’t easy. She persisted, confident that her experience and skills could be applied to each particular challenge in front of her. She has been underestimated repeatedly and forced to prove herself to detractors more often than she can remember. These days she lets her record speak for itself. Tina Stowell has been a peer and member of the House of Lords since January 2011. She was a Cabinet Minister, Leader of the House and the Lord Privy Seal from 2014 to 2016 and was Chair of the Charity Commission from 2018 to 2021. During her time in Parliament, The Spectator Magazine has twice awarded her “Peer of the Year”: in 2024 for successfully changing the law to prevent foreign governments owning or controlling British newspapers; and in 2013 for successfully stewarding the Equal Marriage Act through the second chamber. Recognising how she defied expectations in uniting peers across the House to support this highly contested social change, not only did a right-wing political magazine appreciate her approach in handling landmark legislation, in the same year Stonewall made her their Politician of the Year , as did PinkNews. More recently she has chaired the Communications and Digital Select Committee, where she led a cross-party group of peers reviewing developments in the tech, media and creative industries and advising government on associated public policy, including Artificial Intelligence, digital markets, and the future of the Creative Industries. Tina’s 40-year career has criss-crossed government, politics and the media. She was Head of Corporate Affairs at the BBC, where she spent nearly a decade involved in a wide range of high-profile events, ran William Hague’s office when he was Leader of the Conservative Party, and has been personal PA to Sir David Frost – the late television host and journalist recognised around the world. Earlier in her career she was a civil servant, spending five years in the 10 Downing Street Press Office when John Major was Prime Minister, three years at the British Embassy in Washington DC, and beginning at the Ministry of Defence in Whitehall. In 1996, after leaving the Civil Service at the age of 28, she was awarded the MBE in the Queen’s Birthday Honour’s List for exceptional service. In December 2016 she was proud to receive an Honorary Doctorate from Nottingham University. In recent years, alongside her commitments in Parliament, she has sat on the Boards of ABTA (the travel people) and Impellam Group plc (a group of recruitment and managed services businesses). She has been a Social Mobility Commissioner since 2023. Whatever role she does, Tina’s driving motivation is the same: to make things work better by understanding the people involved and their perspective on the problem that needs to be fixed. Key Achievements Institution of marriage future-proofed: by convincing a huge majority of peers to vote for Equal Marriage legislation .

pages.title: Tina Stowell | Media & Publications
pages.meta_description: Explore Baroness Tina Stowell’s media articles and broadcast appearances on politics, media and social change in modern Britain.
headings: h3: Book Tina to speak at your next event and give your audience clear, practical insights on understanding people, earning trust, and coalition-building.
pages.text_excerpt: Tina Stowell | Media & Publications Home About Keynote Speaker Media & Publications Podcast & Pamphlet Contact Home About Keynote Speaker Media & Publications Podcast & Pamphlet Contact Media & Publications Understanding people. Earning trust. Bridging Divides. Baroness Tina Stowell regularly contributes to national debate through articles, interviews and media appearances. Her writing and commentary span media policy, politics and social change, reflecting her experience across government, broadcasting and public life. She also hosts her own podcast and has authored influential policy work on bridging divides in modern Britain. Book Tina Stowell to speak at your next event Articles in national media New laws for shop staff leave others feeling unprotected The Times – Thursday February 19 2026 Link to Article The BBC must change before its too late The Daily Telegraph – November 2025 Link to Article The Copyright battle is only part of the AI war The Spectator – March 2025 Link to Article Britain’s tech unicorns are galloping overseas Capx – February 2025 Link to Article A two-tier media environment would erode democracy The Times – November 2024 Link to Article The lesson of Gay Marriage we need to re-learn The Spectator – June 2023 Link to Article Arrogance is every politician’s biggest enemy Conservative Home – November 2021 Link to Article Politics needs to change to meet voters’ expectations – and it’s up to the House of Lords to lead the way. Unherd – October 2017 Link to Article Mutual respect is the only way to heal our divided politics Capx – October 2017 Link to Article What Jeremy Corbyn ‘gets’ about Brexit – it’s a catalyst for change The New Statesman – July 2017 Link to Article MEDIA APPEARANCES Tina often appears on national broadcasters such as BBC Radio 4, Times Radio and LBC to discuss and debate topical and political issues – and is often invited to comment on media policy, including the BBC. BBC Radio 4 Week in Westminster Talking about the BBC October 2023, from: 8.10 to 13:55 Link to Programme BBC Radio 4 Week in Westminster Debating about foreign governments owning British newspapers March 2024, from: 10:25 to 17:41 Link to Programme Times Radio Talking about foreign governments owning British newspapers March 2024 Link to Programme Profile Interviews About Tina Roger Bolton’s ‘Beeb Watch’ Podcast January 2023 Link to Podcast Matt Forde’s Political Party Podcast December 2021 Link to Podcast Sunday Times – for ‘Fame and Fortune’ column December 2017 Link to Interview BBC Radio 4 Profile Documentary 2015 Link to Programme Book Tina to speak at your next event and give your audience clear, practical insights on understanding people, earning trust, and coalition-building.
